title: Introduction to Communication Skills
author: Githaka
target_audience: Introductory college students or beginners with no prior background
subject: COMMUNICATION SKILLS
description:
All members within an organization will improve their communication skills if they
follow the communication process, and stay away from the different barriers.
prerequisites: the communicating parties share an area of communicative commonality
for the attainment of organisational goals
the ability to receive, analyse and transmit information in motivating his subordinates in the right direction
that you ask yourself the following questions: (i) Why am I communicating? (ii) What is my real reason for writing, speaking or gesticulating? (iii) W
learning_outcomes: To be heard and to be understood
Be able to communicate effectively in many areas of life, from employment and education to parenting and relationships
Be able to take into account the audience or listener receiving the message and communicate accordingly
Understand how a receiver will process your message
Be able to include visuals, auditory effects as well as kinesthetic information to appeal to the widest range of listeners
Be able to know your audience and use surveys to gather specific information about them
Be able to pay attention to the nonverbal cues of the receiver
Be able to remove barriers to communicating your message
